title: Fix Persistent Login, Logout Button, and Unauthorized Submission Error

requirements.text: Implement persistent customer session in AuthContext so that once a customer logs in, their session is restored automatically on every page load (using localStorage) and they are NOT redirected to the login screen unless they explicitly log out.

quotes: Persistent Login (Stay Logged In): App mein 'Session Persistence' add karo. Customer agar ek baar login karle, toh jab tak wo khud logout na kare, wo logged-in hi rehna chahiye.

acceptanceCriteria: After a customer logs in, refreshing or reopening the app does not show the login screen.
The customer session (user ID, name, mobile, role) is saved to localStorage on successful login.
On app load, AuthContext reads localStorage and restores the customer session if a valid entry exists.
The customer is only sent to the login screen after an explicit logout action.

requirements.text: Fix the Logout button in the Layout component so that clicking it clears the customer or admin session from both AuthContext state and localStorage, then navigates the user back to the login/selection screen.

quotes: Fix Logout Button: Mere app ki 'Logout' button abhi kaam nahi kar rahi hai. Ise fix karo taaki click karne par user sahi se log out ho jaye aur wapas login page par chala jaye.

acceptanceCriteria: Clicking the Logout button clears the user session from AuthContext and localStorage.
After logout, the app navigates to the login/selection screen (LoginSelection or CustomerLogin).
The Logout button is visible and functional for both customer and admin roles.
After logout, no stale session data remains — the user cannot navigate back to protected screens without re-authenticating.

requirements.text: Fix the 'Unauthorized: Only authenticated users can submit orders' error in ServiceForm and any other form submission flows. Ensure that after a customer logs in, the authenticated user's principal or customer ID is correctly attached to every backend call (submitOrder and any similar mutations in useQueries.ts), so forms like 'Niwas Praman Patra' submit successfully without an unauthorized error.

quotes: Submit Error Fix: Jo 'Unauthorized' wala error aa raha tha, use aise theek karo ki ek baar login hone ke baad customer bina kisi rukawat ke 'Niwas Praman Patra' ya koi bhi form submit kar sake.

acceptanceCriteria: A logged-in customer can submit any service form (including Niwas Praman Patra) without receiving an 'Unauthorized' error.
The customer's ID or session token is read from AuthContext and passed correctly in the submitOrder backend call.

The backend actor's submitOrder (or equivalent) function accepts the authenticated customer's ID supplied from the frontend session, not relying solely on Internet Identity principal when the app uses OTP-based custom auth.
If the session is missing or expired at submission time, the user is redirected to login with a clear message instead of showing a raw 'Unauthorized' error.

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
Session persistence must use localStorage only — no external databases or third-party auth services.
All Motoko logic must remain in the single actor file backend/main.mo.
nonGoals: Adding payment, AI chatbot, or order tracking features.
Changing the OTP login mechanism or introducing a new authentication method.
Redesigning the UI layout or visual theme.
Adding new services or form fields.
